title: pseudognaphalium pratense
intro: Pseudognaphalium pratense, also known as white cudweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of white cudweed have been found to provide health benefits such as wound healing, reduction of inflammation, and relief from skin conditions like eczema and acne. White cudweed can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
